meaning: adj.(希腊南部)伯罗奔尼撒半岛的n.伯罗奔尼撒人
examples: Socrates (c. 470–399 B. C. ), who was also a soldier during the Peloponnesian War and a stonemason after, was renowned as a philosopher and educator.
Plato was born during the Peloponnesian War, youth and other children as well aristocratic education, and access to the various trends of thought at that time.
There's a striking moment at the start of Thucydides' "Peloponnesian War" when he surveys Greek history up to then. The striking part is, it only lasts a couple of pages.
Yale historian Donald Kagan, an expert on the Peloponnesian War, was enthusiastic and introduced Strassler to his book agent, Glen Hartley.
Ten years ago Strassler, 69, published The Landmark Thucydides (Free Press), a 713-page edition of the Greek historian's account of the Peloponnesian War.
